title: PortSwigger: SQL Injection

Web Security Academy
NoSQL injection
NoSQL injection
NoSQL injection is a vulnerability where an attacker is able to interfere with the queries that an application makes to a NoSQL database. NoSQL injection may enable an attacker to:
Bypass authentication or protection mechanisms.
Extract or edit data.
Cause a denial of service.
Execute code on the server.
NoSQL databases store and retrieve data in a format other than traditional SQL relational tables. They use a wide range of query languages in
impact: Attacker could exploit sql injection vulnerability
